title: Holland RIASEC Interest Model (O*NET)
description: O*NET uses the Holland Interest Profiler based on six interest types. Students can identify their top 2-3 types to find matching careers.

Realistic.description: Practical, hands-on problem solving. Working with tools, machines, plants, or animals.
Realistic.sampleCareers: Engineering, agriculture, construction, environmental science, forensic science
Realistic.collegeClue: You enjoy lab work, building things, being outdoors, or hands-on projects

Investigative.description: Analytical thinking, research, and intellectual problem solving.
Investigative.sampleCareers: Data science, research, medicine, economics, psychology research
Investigative.collegeClue: You love asking "why?", enjoy research papers, and want to understand how things work

Artistic.description: Creative expression, innovation, and working in unstructured environments.
Artistic.sampleCareers: UX design, marketing creative, journalism, architecture, film production
Artistic.collegeClue: You gravitate toward creative projects, dislike rigid rules, and value self-expression

Social.description: Helping, teaching, counseling, and working with people.
Social.sampleCareers: Counseling, teaching, HR, social work, public health, nursing
Social.collegeClue: You volunteer, enjoy group work, and feel energized by helping others succeed

Enterprising.description: Leading, persuading, managing, and taking business risks.
Enterprising.sampleCareers: Management consulting, sales, entrepreneurship, law, politics
Enterprising.collegeClue: You lead clubs, enjoy debates, and think about starting businesses or organizations

Conventional.description: Organizing, managing data, and following established procedures.
Conventional.sampleCareers: Accounting, financial analysis, project management, database administration
Conventional.collegeClue: You like spreadsheets, planning, organizing events, and making systems more efficient
